claim.observation: fls shows two files named StarFury.zip: an allocated (live) file at Shares/shieldbase-share/R&D/StarFury.zip (inode 120557-128-1) and a deleted file at Windows/Logs/WindowsServerBackup/7.13/R&D/StarFury.zip (inode 124702-128-1). istat of the live copy reports 'Allocated File', Created 2018-07-11 16:16:06 (UTC), File Modified 2012-03-12 20:49:00 (UTC), Actual Size 724919. istat of the deleted copy reports 'Not Allocated File', Created 2018-09-05 13:24:07 (UTC), File Modified 2018-09-06 16:22:02 (UTC), Actual Size 724919.
claim.interpretation: A file with the same name and identical byte size (724919) as the legitimate R&D-share file StarFury.zip was present, deleted, in the WindowsServerBackup\7.13\R&D staging subtree, created 2018-09-05 and deleted 2018-09-06 -- the same window as the M&A research tree and WinRAR tooling. The matching size indicates the staging-tree file is a copy of the genuine R&D share content, showing that the WindowsServerBackup subtree held copies of real file-share data, not just downloaded research. The live R&D-share StarFury.zip (created 2018-07-11, content dated 2012) is still allocated and its timestamps predate the September 2018 activity, so the original share file is intact and is NOT itself an attacker artifact -- only the deleted staging copy is. Identical size is strong but not cryptographic proof of identical content, and this finding does not establish off-host transfer.

evidence_pointer.artifact: base-file-cdrive.E01 -- NTFS volume (offset 0); live inode 120557 (Shares/shieldbase-share/R&D/StarFury.zip) vs deleted inode 124702 (Windows/Logs/WindowsServerBackup/7.13/R&D/StarFury.zip)
evidence_pointer.locator: MFT 120557 (live, allocated) and MFT 124702 (deleted); both Actual Size 724919
evidence_pointer.command: fls -r -p -o 0 IMG | grep StarFury.zip ; istat -o 0 IMG 120557 ; istat -o 0 IMG 124702
